channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Vance’s Trip to Peace Talks on Hold
item.description: Vice President Vance's trip to Pakistan is on hold due to Iran's lack of response to US negotiating positions. The vice president’s trip to Pakistan was suspended because Tehran did not respond to American negotiating positions, a U. S.

item.title: Iran to restart domestic flights; charts phased airspace reopening
item.description: Iranian government sources plan to restart domestic flights, initially allowing overflight operations that will benefit international airlines connecting Europe and Asia, including Indian carriers. Iranian government sources told The Hindu that they plan to first reallow overflight operations that will bring a huge respite to all international airlines connecting Europe with Asia, including Indian carriers.

item.title: Unclear if U.S.-Iran peace talks will happen one day before Trump's latest ultimatum expires
item.description: US-Iran peace talks are uncertain, with a deadline looming one day before Trump's latest ultimatum expires, despite ongoing efforts to reach a deal. Live Updates: Unclear if U. S.-Iran peace talks will happen one day before Trump's latest ultimatum expires CBS News.

item.title: Uncertainty Surrounds U.S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End
item.description: US and Iran talks are uncertain as a cease-fire nears its end. Iran War Live Updates: Uncertainty Surrounds U. S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End The New York Times. Uncertainty Surrounds U. S.-Iran Talks as Cease-Fire Nears End.

item.title: U.S. Turns Up Pressure on Iraq to Distance Itself From Iran
item.description: The US is pressuring Iraq to distance itself from Iran by dismantling Iran-backed militias. Washington is demanding that the Baghdad government dismantle Iran-backed Iraqi militias that have been attacking Americans and U. S.

item.title: Ukraine says Druzhba pipeline running Russian oil to Europe can resume work
item.description: Ukraine has announced that the Druzhba pipeline, which carries Russian oil to Europe, can resume operations, potentially unblocking an EU loan. Kyiv hopeful pipeline restoration will unblock EU loan, with bloc's foreign policy chief expecting 'positive decision'.

item.title: Vance not yet left for Iran talks, still in Washington to participate in meetings, says White House
item.description: Pakistan's Information Minister said the country has yet to receive a formal response on Iranian participation in talks. Meanwhile, over 11.6 lakh Indians have returned from West Asia amid the conflict.

item.title: 2 Americans Killed in Mexico Crash Were C.I.A. Members
item.description: Two Americans killed in a Mexico crash were CIA members returning from a counter-cartel operation. The incident occurred in Chihuahua state, where Mexico's armed forces were leading the operation. The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from a counter-cartel operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.

item.title: Iran won't confirm it will attend peace talks; Trump ready to resume bombing ahead of ceasefire
item.description: Iran's attendance at peace talks is unconfirmed, while US President Trump is prepared to resume bombing ahead of a ceasefire deadline. The situation remains uncertain as the deadline approaches. Live updates: Iran won't confirm it will attend peace talks; Trump ready to resume bombing ahead of ceasefire deadline NBC News.

item.title: Two Palestinians Killed in Israeli Shooting Near West Bank School
item.description: Israeli forces killed two Palestinians, including a child, near a West Bank school in Al Mughayir. The Israeli military said they intervened to break up clashes amid a surge in settler violence.

item.title: Role of US officials killed in crash in Mexico under scrutiny
item.description: Two US officials, reportedly CIA employees, died in a car crash in Mexico following a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

item.title: Trump says U.S. in ‘very, very strong’ position for Iran talks
item.description: US President Trump stated the US is in a "very, very strong" position for Iran talks. He suggested Iran could improve chances of successful peace talks by freeing eight women facing execution.

item.title: Ex-official describes pressure from U.K. PM's office, deepening Mandelson row
item.description: U. K. Prime Minister's office allegedly applied pressure on a former official to justify the appointment of Peter Mandelson, deepening the controversy surrounding the decision. Starmer has since expressed regret over the appointment.

item.title: Israeli sexual violence helping push Palestinians from West Bank: Report
item.description: A report by an NGO found at least 16 cases of conflict-related sexual violence against Palestinians in the West Bank, attributed to Israeli settlers and soldiers. This has contributed to the displacement of Palestinians from the region.

item.title: US "Ready To Go" On War Path. Iran Prepares Fresh Targets, "Surprises"
item.description: Iran has stated it is prepared for war with "new surprises" if necessary. The US has also indicated readiness for conflict, with Iran reportedly preparing fresh targets in response. Tensions have escalated between the two nations.

item.title: Uncertainty clouds Iran talks as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Iran's participation in talks is still uncertain, with Pakistan's Information Minister stating that a formal response is awaited, as a ceasefire deadline nears. As of 7:30 p. m. Pakistan time, a formal response about Iran’s participation “is still awaited”, Pakistan’s Information Minister said in a statement.

item.title: Can Israel’s economy sustain conflicts on multiple fronts?
item.description: Israel's war with Iran cost 35 billion shekels ($11.52 billion) in budgetary expenses. Its operations in Gaza till 2025 will cost around 8.6% of its annual gross domestic product. The Bank of Israel’s preliminary estimates suggest that Israel’s war with Iran came at the cost of 35 billion shekels ($11.52 billion) in budgetary expenses.

item.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
item.description: Iran is experiencing mass redundancies in manufacturers, retailers, and the digital sector due to its war with the US and Israel. The situation may worsen if the war resumes. The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

item.title: 'Unprovoked Acts Of Terror': UAE Minister To NDTV On Iran's Drone Attacks
item.description: UAE Minister Saeed Bin Mubarak Al Hajeri described Iran's drone attacks as "unprovoked terrorist attacks". The strikes caused concern in the region. Saeed Bin Mubarak Al Hajeri described the strikes as "unprovoked terrorist attacks".

item.title: I study international relations. Here’s why the Iran War is heading for a frozen conflict
item.description: The Iran War is heading towards a frozen conflict. This development has implications for international relations. I study international relations. Here’s why the Iran War is heading for a frozen conflict The Independent.
